title: Golden Brown Filipino Vegetable Gratin
description: A hearty, ovo-vegetarian Filipino dish featuring a rich dry white wine sauce and a variety of grilled vegetables, perfect for a comforting meal on a sick day. The terracotta dish adds a rustic touch to this resinous and crumbly gratin.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat and brush the eggplant and zucchini slices with vegetable oil.
Grill the eggplant and zucchini slices for 3-4 minutes per side, or until they are tender and lightly charred.
In a large saucepan, heat the dry white wine over medium heat until it is reduced by half.
Add the minced garlic and chopped onion to the saucepan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the diced tomato to the saucepan and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the grilled eggplant and zucchini slices, the tomato and garlic mixture, and the shredded mozzarella cheese.
Transfer the mixture to a terracotta dish and top with the grated parmesan cheese and breadcrumbs.
Bake the gratin in a preheated oven at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 25-30 minutes, or until it is golden brown and bubbly.
Remove the gratin from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
